chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz
chokepoints.status: Strait reopened June 18 2026 under signed US-Iran agreement. Toll-free transit for 60 days. Shipping cautious — mine-clearing ongoing, traffic normalizing gradually.

chokepoints.name: Bab el-Mandeb
chokepoints.source: ACLED · Lloyd's JWC · Houthi threat active
chokepoints.status: Houthi entry under active consideration. Strategic pause not incapacity.

timeline.hl: Strait of Hormuz vessel traffic drops sharply as US blockade and Iran strikes intensify
timeline.detail: Seven vessels crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, down from 13 the previous day, according to Kpler data. The decline follows the US reimposition of a naval blockade on Iranian ports and Iran's closure announcement on Saturday. The strait carried about a fifth of global oil and gas shipments before the war.

numbers.lbl: Saudi crude on Suezmax tanker exiting Tuesday with transponder off

timeline.hl: U.S. military disables Curaçao-flagged tanker M/T Belma in Arabian Sea with missile strike
timeline.detail: An American fighter jet struck the unladen tanker M/T Belma with a missile on Wednesday (July 15) as it attempted to reach an Iranian port, according to CENTCOM. The tanker ignored multiple warnings while transiting international waters toward Kharg Island. This marks the first time the U.S. has disabled a ship since reimposing a naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this week.

numbers.lbl: compliant vessels redirected during first blockade
non-compliant ships disabled during first blockade
estimated Iranian oil revenue cost from blockade per Pentagon

timeline.src: Antiwar.com citing Axios reporter Barak Ravid · 15 Jul 2026
timeline.hl: Saudi Arabia strikes Sanaa airport after Trump approval, shattering 2022 ceasefire with Houthis.
timeline.detail: U.S. officials told Axios that Trump approved Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman's request for backing a military action against the Houthis. Saudi airstrikes on Sanaa International Airport occurred Monday, July 13, targeting an Iranian flight carrying a Yemeni delegation. In response, Ansar Allah launched missile and drone attacks on a Saudi airport. Mohammed al-Farah of Ansar Allah's political bureau warned oil prices could reach $200 per barrel if escalation closes the Bab al-Mandeb and Strait of Hormuz.

numbers.lbl: Oil price warning if straits closed per Mohammed al-Farah, Ansar Allah
Time since last Iranian aircraft landed at Sanaa airport

timeline.hl: UN condemns escalating attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz; IMO evacuated 11,000 seafarers in June
timeline.detail: IMO and UN human rights chief Volker Türk condemned multiple recent attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz following breakdown of US-Iran ceasefire signed mid-June. IMO had safely evacuated approximately 11,000 seafarers in June but paused operations on 25 June after renewed attacks. Over 20,000 seafarers remain at risk in the region.

numbers.lbl: share of global oil and gas exports via Strait of Hormuz
seafarers evacuated by IMO in June 2026

timeline.hl: Three Liberian-flagged oil tankers attacked off Omani coast; 18 crew evacuated, three missing
timeline.detail: Oman's Maritime Security Center reported Tuesday that three Liberian-flagged oil tankers were targeted in separate incidents off the Omani coast. The Al Bahyah, owned by Al Bahyah Inc. and based in the United Arab Emirates, was targeted approximately 9.6 nautical miles off Musandam Governorate. A Royal Navy of Oman vessel monitored the situation; 18 crew members were evacuated by nearby maritime vessels, while three crew members remained missing.

numbers.lbl: distance of Al Bahyah attack from Musandam Governorate coast

timeline.src: The Epoch Times · 13 Jul 2026; President Donald Trump (Truth Social post)
timeline.hl: Trump announces 20% transit fees for Strait of Hormuz and reimposed Iran blockade
timeline.detail: President Donald Trump announced on July 13 that the United States will impose fees at 20 percent on all cargo shipped through the Strait of Hormuz immediately and will reimpose a blockade on Iranian ports near the Gulf channel. Trump stated the U.S. will be 'THE GUARDIAN OF THE HORMUZ STRAIT' and will be reimbursed at 20 percent on all cargo for providing safety and security.

numbers.lbl: proposed transit fee on Strait of Hormuz cargo

timeline.hl: Strait of Hormuz transit falls to five-week low as US-Iran strikes escalate
timeline.detail: Six vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, the lowest number in five weeks, according to Kpler ship-tracking data. Tankers exiting included the Very Large Crude Carrier Humanity carrying 2 million barrels of Iranian oil and Capetan Andreas carrying 500,000 barrels of Kuwaiti oil products. Most tankers switched off transponders during transit. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said its navy stopped two ships by shutting down their systems.

numbers.lbl: Kuwaiti oil products aboard Capetan Andreas

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after striking container ship; US retaliates with military strikes
timeline.detail: Iran said it closed the Strait of Hormuz after firing a warning shot that struck the GFS Galaxy, a Cyprus-flagged container ship, causing significant engine-room damage. US Central Command launched strikes after Iran's statement. Iran's Revolutionary Guards said the strait will remain closed until 'the end of US interference in this region.' Iran's blockade has caused energy prices to surge, fuelling global inflation.

numbers.lbl: share of world oil and LNG supplies previously transiting the Strait before the war

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after striking container ship; US retaliates with strikes
timeline.detail: US Central Command said it launched strikes on Iran at 7:15 p.m. ET on Saturday, ordered by Trump, following the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps striking the M/V GFS Galaxy, a Cyprus-flagged container ship, in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Iran said it closed the vital strait after firing a warning shot at vessels on an unapproved route and warned any retaliation would be met with a severe response. The strait previously conveyed one-fifth of the world's oil and LNG supplies.

numbers.lbl: share of global oil and LNG supplies transiting Strait of Hormuz before war

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after missile exchanges with U.S., Gulf states targeted
timeline.detail: Iran said it closed the Strait of Hormuz after firing a warning shot that struck a vessel on an unapproved route and disabled a second vessel on Sunday. Iran's Revolutionary Guards stated the strait will remain closed until the end of U.S. interference in the region. U.S. Central Command reported hitting 140 Iranian military targets on Saturday out of more than 300 during three nights of strikes. Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, and Jordan reported Iranian attacks on military facilities, with Qatar's government saying three people including a child were injured by falling shrapnel.

numbers.lbl: Iranian military targets hit by U.S. on Saturday
Iranian targets hit during three nights of U.S. strikes
Share of world oil and LNG shipments transiting strait before war

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz after striking container ship; U.S. launches retaliatory strikes
timeline.detail: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck the M/V GFS Galaxy, a Cyprus-flagged container ship, in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday, causing significant engine-room damage and a missing civilian crew member. Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed and warned of a severe response to any retaliation. U.S. Central Command launched strikes on Iran at 7:15 p.m. ET in response. The closure follows U.S. and Iran attacks over several days, prompting President Trump to declare an end to a ceasefire that began February 28.

numbers.lbl: proportion of world oil and LNG supplies transited through Strait of Hormuz before war

timeline.hl: Oman proposes draft plan for Strait of Hormuz transit with separate corridors for Iranian and international vessels
timeline.detail: CNN reported that Oman made a draft proposal for the Strait of Hormuz including free navigation through its southern corridor in Omani territorial waters. The plan calls for vessels transiting the northern corridor through Iranian territorial waters to obtain prior approval from Iran, with no tolls imposed. Omani and Iranian negotiators agreed to continue talks at technical and political levels.

timeline.hl: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed after unauthorised vessel hit; tanker attacks escalate
timeline.detail: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed following an unauthorised vessel strike. Three Qatari and Saudi commercial tankers came under fire earlier in the week, prompting US strikes on Iranian sites and Iranian retaliatory strikes on US military sites in Gulf states. The US revoked the license authorizing sale of Iranian crude on Tuesday after the vessels were hit.

numbers.lbl: world oil supply transiting Strait of Hormuz before war

timeline.hl: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed after unauthorised vessel hit; Oman proposes draft corridor plan
timeline.detail: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed following an unauthorised vessel incident. CNN reported that Oman drafted a proposal allowing free navigation through its southern corridor in Omani waters, while vessels in the northern corridor through Iranian waters would require prior Iranian approval without tolls. Three Qatari and Saudi commercial tankers came under fire earlier in the week, prompting US strikes on Iranian sites and Iranian retaliation on US military sites in Gulf states.

numbers.lbl: world's oil supply transited through Strait of Hormuz before the war

timeline.hl: Oman proposes draft plan for Strait of Hormuz transit with separate corridors for free and restricted passage
timeline.detail: CNN reported that Oman made a draft proposal for the Strait of Hormuz including free navigation through its southern corridor in Omani territorial waters. The plan called for vessels in the northern corridor through Iranian territorial waters to obtain prior approval from Iran, with no tolls imposed. Omani and Iranian negotiators agreed to continue talks at technical and political levels.

timeline.hl: Strait of Hormuz reopened late June after closure; traffic slowed again this week following US-Iran attacks
timeline.detail: The Strait of Hormuz reopened in late June, enabling rival Middle Eastern producers to resume exports. However, traffic through the vital waterway slowed again this week after tit-for-tat attacks between the US and Iran. Between June 15 and July 6, about 30 million barrels of Iranian oil were loaded, equivalent to 1.35 million barrels per day, according to tanker tracker Vortexa Analytics.

timeline.hl: IEA warns US-Iran escalation could upend oil surplus forecast; Gulf exports down 33% from pre-war levels
timeline.detail: IEA reports global oil supply rebounded 4.1 mb/d to 98.8 mb/d in June, but remains 9.4 mb/d below pre-war levels. Gulf total exports increased 6.5 mb/d MoM to 16.1 mb/d in June versus 24 mb/d pre-war average. IEA forecast supply will decline to 102.6 mb/d in 2026 contingent on swift de-escalation. War insurance for ships in the Gulf reached 3% of vessel value, up from 2% end of prior week, with quotes as high as 5% circulating.

numbers.lbl: War insurance rate for Gulf ships

timeline.hl: Iran demands sole control of Strait of Hormuz and payment fees from vessels
timeline.detail: Tehran's UN diplomat stated that any activity in the Strait of Hormuz, including its opening or demining operations, rests exclusively with Iran. Iran has demanded the strait be under its sole control and that vessels pay fees to Tehran, despite the waterway being considered international for decades. About a fifth of all traded oil and natural gas passed through the strait before the war began.

numbers.lbl: share of traded oil and natural gas through Strait of Hormuz before war

timeline.hl: Iran claims sole control of Strait of Hormuz, refuses U.S. demand for public statement on passage
timeline.detail: U.S. officials demanded Iran issue a public statement declaring the Strait of Hormuz open and that ships crossing it won't be attacked. Tehran has refused, instead insisting the route remain under Iranian control and that Iran be allowed to charge vessels transiting it. Iran's UN diplomat stated any activity in the strait 'rests exclusively with Iran.'

timeline.hl: OPEC+ approves 188,000 bpd production increase for August across seven members
timeline.detail: Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman will raise combined production by 188,000 barrels per day in August. OPEC+ stated the countries will continue monitoring market conditions and reaffirmed the importance of adopting a cautious approach to support market stability. This marks the fifth consecutive monthly increase in output.

Brent crude peak price in March 2026

timeline.hl: OPEC+ approves 188,000 bpd August production increase from seven members
timeline.detail: Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman agreed to raise combined production by 188,000 barrels per day in August, marking the fifth consecutive monthly increase. OPEC+ stated the countries will continue monitoring market conditions and adopt a cautious approach to support market stability.

timeline.hl: OPEC+ approves 188,000 bpd production increase for August with seven members participating
timeline.detail: OPEC+ announced on July 5 that Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman would raise combined production by 188,000 barrels per day in August. The group stated the countries would continue monitoring market conditions and reaffirmed the importance of a cautious approach to support market stability. This marks the fifth consecutive monthly increase in output.

timeline.hl: Seven OPEC+ members to increase oil output by 188,000 barrels per day
timeline.detail: Seven OPEC+ members, including Saudi Arabia and Russia, announced plans to expand monthly oil production. The increase amounts to 188,000 barrels per day according to Al Jazeera.

numbers.lbl: Production increase by seven OPEC+ members

timeline.hl: Seven OPEC+ countries agree to increase oil production by 188,000 bpd in August
timeline.detail: OPEC+ announced on 5 July that Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman would expand combined oil production by 188,000 barrels per day in August. This was the fifth consecutive month OPEC+ agreed to raise oil outputs. The group stated countries would continue monitoring market conditions and adopt a cautious approach to support market stability.

numbers.lbl: Brent crude peak price in March 2026

timeline.hl: OPEC+ seven countries to increase oil production by 188,000 bpd in August
timeline.detail: OPEC+ announced on Sunday that Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman would expand oil production by a combined total of 188,000 barrels per day in August. This marks the fifth consecutive month OPEC+ agreed to raise oil outputs. The decision came as crude oil prices declined following a US-Iran interim deal.

numbers.lbl: Brent crude price at Sunday night open

timeline.hl: Seven OPEC+ countries to increase combined oil production by 188,000 bpd in August
timeline.detail: OPEC+ announced on Sunday that Saudi Arabia, Russia, Iraq, Kuwait, Kazakhstan, Algeria and Oman would expand oil production by a combined total of 188,000 barrels per day in August. This marks the fifth consecutive month OPEC+ agreed to raise oil outputs. The decision follows crude oil price declines to levels not seen since before the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran.

timeline.src: ZeroHedge · 6 July 2026 (citing Iranian Ambassador Abdolreza Rahmani Fazli speech at World Peace Forum in Beijing)
timeline.hl: Iran plans service fees for Hormuz transit with special rates for China and friendly nations
timeline.detail: Iranian Ambassador Abdolreza Rahmani Fazli stated Iran will impose service fees on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, with China and other friendly countries receiving special considerations on fee levels and types. Fees are framed as charges for security, administration, and environmental management rather than tolls. Iran is working with Oman on new transit arrangements for the strait.

numbers.lbl: share of world oil exports through Hormuz before recent conflict
approximate share of Iran's oil exports purchased by China
period for free commercial transit under June 15 Iran-US MoU

timeline.hl: US lifts sanctions on Iranian oil exports for 60 days until August 21, 2026
timeline.detail: The United States temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil and petrochemical exports for 60 days until August 21, 2026. The waiver covers banking, insurance and shipping linked to Iranian oil exports and allows Iran to transact with international partners and receive payments in dollars. Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi stated that oil and petrochemical exports are waived, the blockade is lifted, and frozen assets are being released.

numbers.lbl: Iranian crude oil exports through Strait of Hormuz week of June 15

timeline.hl: US waives Iran sanctions for 60 days following peace talks; tanker traffic resumes in Strait of Hormuz
timeline.detail: The United States waived sanctions on Iran for 60 days from June 23, 2026, following talks in Switzerland on a nascent peace deal. The waiver, announced by the U.S. Treasury and effective until August 21, allows Iran to sell oil and related products and receive payment. Tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz started to pick up on Monday following agreement on a communications mechanism for safe commercial passage.

timeline.hl: Iran-U.S. 60-day negotiating period established; Iran agrees not to charge tolls during talks
timeline.detail: A memorandum of understanding allows Iran to manage the Strait of Hormuz while holding discussions with Oman and six other Gulf states to define future administration. Iran agreed not to charge transiting vessels tolls for 60 days. President Trump suggested the U.S. might impose its own tolls if a final deal is not reached during the negotiating period.

numbers.lbl: days for negotiating period and toll moratorium

timeline.src: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation CEO (via Kuwait News Agency) · 22 Jun 2026
timeline.hl: Kuwait expects oil output to reach 2 million bpd within a week following Strait of Hormuz reopening
timeline.detail: KPC CEO stated Kuwait expects output to reach 2 million bpd within a week. Production had collapsed to 573,000 bpd in May following Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz in late February. The Strait reopened following a memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran.

numbers.lbl: Kuwait expected oil output within one week
Shut-in Middle Eastern production available for restoration

timeline.hl: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz; tanker transit plummets from 26 to 5 ships in one day
timeline.detail: Data from analytics firm Kpler showed five ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz on June 21, down from 26 ships on June 20. Iran declared the waterway closed on June 20 in retaliation for Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Three ships were identified as very large crude carriers (VLCC) each holding 2 million barrels of Saudi crude and fuel oil, with one heading to Japan.

numbers.lbl: world oil and gas supplies normally transiting strait prewar

timeline.hl: Iran lifts Strait blockade after cease-fire extension, then reimposed by IRGC
timeline.detail: Tehran lifted its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after agreeing with the US to extend a cease-fire announced in April for 60 days to allow peace negotiations. On June 20, the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps declared the waterway closed in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Kpler data showed five ships crossed on June 21, down from 26 ships on June 20, including three VLCCs carrying 2 million barrels each of Saudi crude and fuel oil.

numbers.lbl: vessels crossing Strait on June 21 per Kpler
vessels crossing Strait on June 20 per Kpler
world oil and gas supplies transiting Strait prewar

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again, citing US failure to halt Lebanon fighting
timeline.detail: Iran announced it had again closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing US failure to meet commitments to halt fighting in Lebanon under a memorandum of understanding reached the previous week. A US diplomat told Axios that during talks in Switzerland, parties made good progress on keeping the strait open and focused on enforcing the ceasefire in Lebanon and the nuclear deal.

numbers.lbl: duration of extended ceasefire under new agreement

timeline.hl: Four Qatari LNG tankers enter Strait of Hormuz after Iran ceasefire agreement
timeline.detail: Four liquefied natural gas tankers controlled by Qatar (Wadi Al Sail, Mekaines, Al Sadd, Mesaimeer) entered the Strait of Hormuz on 22 June 2026 via the Iranian route for the first time since the US-Israeli war with Iran began on 28 February 2025, ship-tracking data from Kpler showed. This occurred after Iran lifted its blockade following a 60-day ceasefire extension agreement with the US, though Tehran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps declared the waterway shut again on Saturday in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon.

numbers.lbl: oil transited on 20 Jun 2026 per US Central Command

timeline.hl: Strait of Hormuz closure costs Iraq $37 billion; pre-crisis output 4.2 million bpd.
timeline.detail: Regional war and Strait of Hormuz closure have pushed Iraq into a severe economic crisis. Financial and energy analysts estimate Iraq's losses at more than $37 billion. Before the crisis, Iraq produced more than 4.2 million barrels per day, including approximately 3.5 million barrels exported internationally. Energy experts caution Iraqi oil fields may require months to return to prewar output levels.

numbers.lbl: Estimated Iraqi losses from Hormuz closure
Iraq's pre-crisis oil exports to international markets

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again citing US failures in Lebanon ceasefire enforcement.
timeline.detail: Iran announced over the weekend of 20-21 June 2026 that it had again closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing US failure to meet commitments to halt fighting in Lebanon. The closure occurred as Vice President JD Vance met Iranian officials at Buergenstock, Switzerland for first talks under interim peace deal reached the previous week extending ceasefire from April for at least 60 days.

timeline.src: AOG Digital · 22 Jun 2026; Kpler shipping data; U.S. Central Command
timeline.hl: Four Qatari LNG tankers transit Hormuz after Iran declares waterway closed
timeline.detail: Four Qatari LNG carriers—Wadi Al Sail, Mekaines, Al Sadd and Mesaimeer—entered the Strait of Hormuz on Monday via the Iranian route for the first time since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began February 28, despite Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps declaring the waterway shut on Saturday. U.S. Central Command reported 55 merchant ships transited Saturday carrying more than 17 million barrels of oil. Kpler data showed five vessels passed Sunday, down from 26 the day prior.

numbers.lbl: oil volume transited Saturday per U.S. Central Command

timeline.hl: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again; ship traffic plunges to 5 vessels from 26
timeline.detail: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps closed the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon, citing violations of an interim peace deal. Kpler shipping data showed only 5 vessels passed the strait on Sunday, down from 26 on Saturday. The vessels included three VLCCs carrying 2 million barrels of Saudi crude and fuel oil each, with one heading to Japan.

numbers.lbl: ships that passed Strait of Hormuz on Sunday
ships that passed Strait of Hormuz on Saturday
capacity per VLCC carrying Saudi crude

timeline.src: US Central Command (CENTCOM) · 8 Jun 2026; MEA statement · 9 Jun 2026
timeline.hl: US forces disable Palau-flagged tanker MT Marivex in Gulf of Oman; 24 Indian crew rescued
timeline.detail: US Central Command announced that US forces disabled the Palau-flagged oil tanker MT Marivex in the Gulf of Oman on June 8 after the crew failed to comply with directions from US forces. An F/A-18 Super Hornet from USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the ship's engineering and steering spaces. The vessel was allegedly attempting to sail toward an Iranian port in violation of a US-led blockade. All 24 Indian crew members were rescued safely with assistance from Omani authorities.

numbers.lbl: Indian crew members rescued from MT Marivex

timeline.src: US Central Command (CENTCOM) · 8 Jun 2026; MEA spokesperson Jaiswal · 9 Jun 2026
timeline.hl: US forces disable Palau-flagged tanker MT Marivex in Gulf of Oman; 24 Indian crew rescued
timeline.detail: US Central Command announced that US forces disabled the Palau-flagged oil tanker MT Marivex in the Gulf of Oman on June 8 after the crew failed to comply with directions from US forces. An F/A-18 Super Hornet from USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the ship's engineering and steering spaces. The vessel was allegedly attempting to sail toward an Iranian port in violation of the US-led blockade against Iran. All 24 Indian crew members aboard were rescued safely with assistance from Omani authorities.

timeline.src: US Central Command (CENTCOM) · 8 Jun 2026; MEA Spokesperson Jaiswal · 9 Jun 2026
timeline.hl: US forces disable Palau-flagged tanker MT Marivex in Gulf of Oman; 24 Indian crew rescued
timeline.detail: MT Marivex, a Palau-flagged oil tanker, was disabled by US forces in the Gulf of Oman on 8 June after crew failed to comply with US directions. CENTCOM stated the vessel was allegedly attempting to sail toward an Iranian port in violation of a US-led blockade. An F/A-18 Super Hornet from USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the ship's engineering and steering spaces. All 24 Indian nationals aboard were rescued safely with assistance from Omani authorities.

timeline.src: Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal · 9 June 2026
timeline.hl: US Navy disables Palau-flagged tanker MT Marivex in Gulf of Oman after crew non-compliance
timeline.detail: The Palau-flagged oil tanker MT Marivex was disabled by US forces in the Gulf of Oman on 8 June 2026 after the crew failed to comply with directions from US Navy. An F/A-18 Super Hornet from USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the ship's engineering and steering spaces. CENTCOM stated the vessel was allegedly attempting to sail toward an Iranian port in violation of the US-led blockade. All 24 Indian crew members aboard were rescued safely with assistance from Omani authorities.

timeline.hl: US strikes Iran after Army Apache helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
timeline.detail: An Army AH-64 Apache attack helicopter collided with an Iranian drone and crashed off Oman's coast during patrol over the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the two aviators were rescued by drone boat at 3:30 a.m. local time Tuesday, approximately two hours after the crash. President Trump stated both service members are safe and uninjured. In response, U.S. Central Command said strikes on Iran would be a proportional response to Iranian aggression. Iranian state media reported explosions on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz.

timeline.hl: U.S. military strikes Iran after Army Apache helicopter crash near Strait of Hormuz
timeline.detail: U.S. Central Command carried out strikes on Iran following the crash of an Army AH-64 Apache attack helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The helicopter collided with an Iranian drone, according to a U.S. official. Iranian state media reported explosions on Qeshm Island. U.S. Central Command described the strikes as a proportional response to Iranian aggression.

timeline.hl: Iran seizes two container ships in Hormuz. Vance cancels Islamabad. Ceasefire extended.
timeline.detail: Iran's Revolutionary Guard seizes two container ships crossing without authorisation. Vance cancels Islamabad trip after Tehran says it won't participate. Trump extends ceasefire citing 'seriously fractured' Iranian leadership — truce continues until Iran presents a 'unified proposal'. Iran states it will not reopen Hormuz while US Navy intercepts vessels. Brent ~$102.

timeline.hl: Iran declares Hormuz 'completely open'. Oil falls 12% to $83. Then walks it back.
timeline.detail: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi announces Hormuz open to all commercial vessels during Lebanon ceasefire. Oil falls nearly 12% to ~$83/bbl. Trump says US blockade stays until negotiations conclude. Iran then cancels reopening after US maintains naval presence. Video footage shows ships turning away. April 19: Iranian parliament announces plans to legislate tolls from all non-hostile nations.

timeline.hl: US naval blockade of Iranian ports begins. Brent above $100. Mine-clearing underway.
timeline.detail: US blockade takes effect at 10am ET targeting all vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports. CENTCOM clarifies freedom of navigation for non-Iranian ports is not impeded. OPEC March output fell 27% to 20.79M bpd — largest monthly decline since 2020. Morgan Stanley maintains $110/bbl Q2 forecast. Saudi Arabia announces partial restoration of EW pipeline and Manifa production.

timeline.src: Al Jazeera · Wall Street Journal · April 12 2026
timeline.hl: Islamabad talks fail. Trump announces US naval blockade of Iranian ports.
timeline.detail: US Navy destroyers enter Hormuz for mine-clearing. Iran warns military vessels approaching are a ceasefire violation. Vance leaves Islamabad April 12 with no agreement — US accused Iran of refusing to curb nuclear ambitions; Iran sought Hormuz sovereignty, war reparations, and a regional ceasefire. Trump immediately announces blockade effective April 13.

timeline.src: Reuters · Bloomberg · Saudi Press Agency · April 8–9 2026
timeline.hl: Ceasefire agreed then violated. EW pipeline hit. Saudi facilities struck. 230 tankers stranded.
timeline.detail: Ceasefire brokered by Pakistan on April 8. Hours later Saudi Arabia's East-West pipeline pumping station struck — throughput falls 700K bpd. Manifa and Khurais oilfields hit (−600K bpd combined). SATORP Jubail, Ras Tanura refinery, SAMREF Yanbu and Riyadh refinery also struck. Only 5–9 vessels transit Hormuz in first 24hrs vs prewar average of 100+. 230 loaded tankers stranded in Gulf.

timeline.hl: First Iraqi crude tanker transits Hormuz since war began. SOMO requests lifting schedules.
timeline.detail: Iraqi-owned vessel Ocean Thunder — loaded ~1M bbl Basrah Heavy on March 3, stranded in Gulf throughout March — transits Hormuz April 5. Expected at Malaysia's Pengerang refinery around April 18. SOMO requests lifting schedules from buyers April 7, noting all Basra terminals are 'fully operational'. Kpler estimates ~20M bbl floating storage and 17M bbl onshore stocks ready to move. 3-month recovery timeline if Hormuz reopens fully.

timeline.hl: Iran grants selective Hormuz access to five nations. Charging tolls over $1M per ship.
timeline.detail: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi announces ships from China, Russia, India, Iraq and Pakistan may transit under Iranian armed forces coordination. Malaysia and Thailand gain access days later. Iran also agrees to allow humanitarian and fertiliser shipments March 27. Iran simultaneously demands shipping firms pay tolls in cryptocurrency — Trump condemns this as 'world extortion'.

timeline.hl: Indian refiners challenge OSP benchmark — 30-year pricing architecture under stress.
timeline.detail: Indian refiners approach Aramco to shift OSP benchmark from Oman/Dubai to ICE Brent, saying they are paying $45–50/bbl more due to disrupted Platts assessment. Direct challenge to pricing architecture underpinning Gulf crude trade since the 1980s. Two-thirds of Indian LPG demand remains blocked.

timeline.hl: Iraq declares force majeure. Production collapses to ~875K bpd — largest single-country shut-in.
timeline.detail: Baghdad cites war-related disruptions as force majeure trigger. Production cut from 4.25M bpd to ~875K bpd. All produced crude directed to domestic refineries. Iraq's oil revenue funds 90% of the national budget. 90% of Iraq's imports of food, goods and medicine also transit Hormuz.

timeline.hl: IEA OMR: global supply to fall 8 mb/d in March. Largest supply disruption in history.
timeline.detail: IEA formally declares largest supply disruption in history of the global oil market. Brent at ~$92/bbl — up $20/bbl for the month. Global observed inventories stood at 8,210 mb in January.

timeline.hl: IEA releases 400 mb from emergency reserves — largest coordinated release in history.
timeline.detail: IEA member countries unanimously agree to release 400 million barrels — the largest coordinated emergency stock release ever undertaken, covering approximately 40 days of lost Gulf supply.

timeline.src: IEA OMR · Saudi Aramco CEO · Kpler
timeline.hl: Yanbu hits record 5.9 mb/d exports. VLCC rates 6× five-year average.
timeline.detail: Saudi Arabia achieves unprecedented pipeline rerouting from Gulf to Red Sea coast. France announces naval deployment. 40 VLCCs — 78 mb of oil — stranded in Gulf.

timeline.hl: Iran attempts to strike Shaybah — first attack 500km inside Saudi territory.
timeline.detail: Saudi officials report interception of drones targeting the 1 mb/d Shaybah field near the UAE border. Saudi Arabia begins routing flows via East-West pipeline to Yanbu at record pace.

timeline.src: IEA OMR · Saudi Aramco · Kuwait Oil Co
timeline.hl: Gulf producers begin systematic production cuts as storage fills.
timeline.detail: Kuwait starts curtailing production — 14 days of storage exhausted. Bahrain scales back after drone strike on Sitra refinery. Offshore fields across the region forced to halt.

timeline.src: IEA OMR · SOMO Iraq · QatarEnergy · WTO/AXSMarine
timeline.hl: Iraq curtails. QatarEnergy shuts Ras Laffan. WTO/AXSMarine confirms near-total Hormuz halt.
timeline.detail: Iraq cites storage constraints for 700 kb/d cuts at Rumaila. QatarEnergy shuts LNG liquefaction — 1.1 mb/d condensate and NGL offline, one-third of global helium supply disrupted. WTO/AXSMarine Hormuz Trade Tracker confirms outbound crude fell to near zero on February 28 with simultaneous halts across LNG and fertiliser.

timeline.hl: Tanker traffic through Hormuz collapses to near zero.
timeline.detail: Ship owners and charterers halt loadings. Storage tanks in Gulf ports begin filling. OPEC+ emergency meeting supports April target increase of 206 kb/d — academic given shutdown. Maersk, CMA CGM and Hapag-Lloyd suspend transits.

timeline.hl: US and Israel launch joint air strikes on Iran. War begins. Hormuz effectively closed.
timeline.detail: Brent futures surge toward $120/bbl. Tanker operators begin refusing Hormuz transit. At least three tankers struck near the strait. Maritime insurance rates immediately suspended.

saudi_arabia.hl: EW pipeline pumping station struck April 8. −700K bpd throughput. Manifa + Khurais hit (−600K bpd). Four refineries attacked. Partial recovery announced April 12.
US naval blockade of Iranian ports effective April 13. Iranian mines in strait. Near-zero tanker transits since March 1.
Yanbu hit record 5.9M bbl/d exports on March 9. Pipeline rerouting strategy was working before April 8 attack.
Arab Light OSP for April 2026 loading: $84.00/bbl vs Oman/Dubai differential. Brent now ~$102.

iraq.hl: Iran seizes two container ships in Hormuz. Brent ~$102. Vance cancels Islamabad. Ceasefire extended by Trump citing 'fractured' Tehran.
Iraq opens Rabia-Yarubiyah border crossing with Syria for overland exports after 13 years. Emergency measure — trucking volumes negligible vs marine.
First Iraqi crude tanker (Ocean Thunder, ~1M bbl Basrah Heavy) transits Hormuz April 5. SOMO requests lifting schedules. ~20M bbl floating storage ready.
Force majeure declared on all foreign-operated oilfields. Production cut from 4.25M to ~875K bbl/d. Largest single-country shut-in of the crisis.

saudi_arabia.production_note: bbl/day · Manifa + Khurais struck · SPA April 9

iraq.budget_share_note: of government budget · IMF / World Bank
